params.prompt: Transform this room image into a cinematic editorial real-estate still. Preserve exact architecture, perspective, walls, windows, door openings, furniture layout, lens realism, and room scale. Maintain believable residential scale for a 9.333333333333334 by 12 ft Dining (Dine). Apply one editorial image-pass to define bay-window geometry and to emphasize the pendant as the visible practical. Accent the bay seat or ceiling reveal with a subtle amber strip for evening mood. Keep exterior bay glazing as primary daylight; balance the pendant so it doesn't clip highlights. A refined, low-contrast monochrome palette layered with warm wood and brass accents. The overall direction is meditative and textural: matte black walls and smoked oak slatted panels anchor low-profile furniture, amber cove and recessed strip lights provide warm contrast to exterior daylight, and sparse sculptural objects (bonsai, ceramic vessels, a single bookshelf) give the scenes a calm, curated feel. Maintain architectural fidelity, keep verticals true, and derive all light from visible windows and practical fixtures. Keep these fixed unless the user explicitly wants transformation: exact architecture room geometry furniture layout lens realism vertical line integrity believable scale light entering from visible sources only VISUAL CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENT (reference image provided): The dining table, pendant light above the dining area, dining chairs, bay exterior glazing, smoked-oak dining table, satin-brass pendant (visible), amber strip in bay reveal, smoked oak dining table, satin-brass pendant (single), bay window seating or reveal with amber strip, minimal tabletop vessel in this room must exactly match the style, materials, and form shown in reference image — treat it as the definitive source of truth for these pieces. The Taste (Kitchen) visible through the open kitchen must show exactly the same kitchen cabinets, range hood, kitchen island or counter, matte black cabinetry, smoked-oak open shelving with curated books, under-cabinet warm LED strip, satin-brass pendant (visible practical), open smoked-oak shelving, bookshelf niche with curated volumes as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. The Nest visible through the open-plan living area must show exactly the same sofa, coffee table, area rug, bay exterior glazing with directional daylight, smoked-oak vertical slat wall with amber cove, charcoal low sectional, monolithic black stone coffee table, bookshelf (sparse), visible floor lamp, smoked oak vertical slat wall with amber cove, bonsai (single), bookshelf as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. Previous failure modes to correct: wrong_room_type, lighting_drift, object_missing. The still image generally conveys the intended design aesthetic, but it does not fully satisfy the requirements for this specific dining room type, particularly in object completeness and lighting realism. The bay window details and specific furnishings need better representation.
